prompt:
Document-model ticket for the outline editor. The first prototype of our section tree stored parent pointers as Rc<Node> — parent and child kept each other alive, and closing a document leaked every node, every time, silently. Memory graphs only went up. We ripped it out; this is the do-over, and this time the ownership rule is law: CHILDREN ARE OWNED STRONGLY, PARENTS ARE Weak. The acceptance tests literally assert Rc::strong_count / Rc::weak_count at pinned values after every structural operation, plus drop-the-root-and-watch-every-Weak-die, so the design can't quietly regress.

Crate rs-rcweaktree (lib name rs_rcweaktree; Cargo.toml and .gitignore are in place, hands off both). Stdlib only, single-threaded on purpose — this is the editor's UI-thread model, Rc not Arc. Contract: tests/rcweaktree.rs untouched, `cargo test --offline` green.

API (all on `pub struct Node`, which the tests only ever hold as Rc<Node>):

- `Node::new(label: &str) -> Rc<Node>`
- `label(&self) -> &str`
- `parent(&self) -> Option<Rc<Node>>` — upgrade of the weak link; None for roots
- `children(&self) -> Vec<Rc<Node>>` — snapshot, insertion order
- `path_to_root(&self) -> Vec<String>` — labels from self up to and including the root
- `Node::attach(parent: &Rc<Node>, child: &Rc<Node>) -> Result<(), TreeError>`
- `detach(&self) -> bool` — unhook from parent; false if there wasn't one
- `Node::reparent(node: &Rc<Node>, new_parent: &Rc<Node>) -> Result<(), TreeError>`
- `Node::preorder(root: &Rc<Node>) -> Vec<Rc<Node>>` — self first, then each child's subtree, children in insertion order
- `Node::find(root: &Rc<Node>, label: &str) -> Option<Rc<Node>>` — first preorder match within that subtree only
- `#[derive(Debug, PartialEq, Eq)] pub enum TreeError { AlreadyAttached, WouldCycle }`

Rules:

- attach appends to the parent's child list and points the child's weak link back. A child that already has a live parent: Err(AlreadyAttached), nothing modified, no duplicate slots. Attaching a node to itself or to any node in its own subtree (i.e. the prospective parent is, or descends from, the child): Err(WouldCycle). When both objections apply, WouldCycle is the answer — check the cycle first.
- detach removes exactly one strong slot from the parent and clears the weak link (counts drop to pre-attach values — tests check). Detaching a root, or a node whose parent has already been dropped, returns false and touches nothing.
- reparent = cycle-check against the new parent, then detach from wherever it is (if anywhere), then append to the new parent. Reparenting under the node itself or its own descendant: Err(WouldCycle), structure byte-for-byte unchanged. Reparenting to the parent it already has is legal and moves it to the BACK of the child list. Reparenting a detached node is a plain attach. Subtrees ride along whole.
- A node whose parent was dropped out from under it behaves as a root: parent() None, path_to_root just itself, detach false. No panics, no unwraps on dead Weaks.
- path_to_root/preorder/find must not mutate anything and must not bump counts once their return values are dropped.

No interior-mutability gymnastics beyond what the design needs (RefCell is expected; Mutex is not). No unsafe.
